response: Originating from 1960s U.S. defense research and the 1969 ARPANET demonstration, the internet evolved through foundational milestones like the 1983 TCP/IP standard and Tim Berners-Lee’s 1989 invention of the World Wide Web. After gaining mainstream accessibility in the 1990s and surviving the 2001 dot-com crash, it expanded rapidly through social media, 2007 smartphone adoption, and cloud computing. Today, this once-niche research network connects over 5 billion people globally, serving as the indispensable infrastructure that underpins modern commerce, communication, and daily life.
